description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular, slightly rounded wooden or Bakelite casing. The front face features a large speaker grille covered with a mesh or fabric, flanked by two prominent circular knobs likely used for tuning and volume control. Below the speaker grille, there is a horizontal frequency indicator window, possibly with a glass or plastic cover, displaying tuning information. The back of the radio is flat and plain, with visible screws and a power cord extending from the lower section, indicating its electrical nature. The sides and top are smooth and unadorned, showcasing the rich brown color and subtle sheen of the casing material. This radio receiver is compact and designed to sit on a flat surface, such as a table or shelf, in an urban environment.
description: A compact, vintage tabletop radio receiver with a brown, rounded rectangular casing, front speaker grille, and two control knobs.
description_view_0: Side view showing the smooth, curved brown casing and the rectangular base, with minimal visible features.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the speaker grille, two circular control knobs, and a horizontal frequency indicator window.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, highlighting the unadorned, curved casing.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a flat back panel with screws and a visible power cord extending from the lower section.
